---
name: content-voice
description: Rewrite portfolio copy in a consistent voice and strip AI slop (banned phrases, em dashes, emoji, adjective-heavy claims). Use when rewriting hero text, about sections, project descriptions, or any copy that sounds generic, sycophantic, or AI-generated.
---

# content-voice

Apply a defined voice to portfolio copy and remove AI slop.

## When to use
- Copy was generated by an AI tool and reads generic
- User has voice keywords but the copy doesn't reflect them
- Copy contains banned phrases, em dashes, or emoji in body content

## Inputs
- Existing copy (hero, about, project descriptions)
- 3-5 voice keywords (e.g., direct, curious, technical-but-warm)
- Optional: 2-3 sample sentences in the desired voice

## Process
1. Strip banned phrases entirely — no replacement. See `./banned-phrases.md` for the full dictionary.
2. Replace adjective-heavy claims with verbs + outcomes. *"Passionate engineer"* → *"shipped 3 ML pipelines"*.
3. Cut em dashes. Use commas, periods, or parentheses.
4. Remove emoji from body copy.
5. Apply voice keywords as constraints — each paragraph must reflect at least one.
6. Read-aloud test: any sentence that sounds like generic AI output gets rewritten.

## Output format
- Rewritten copy, section by section
- A short "changes" note explaining the biggest 2-3 edits
- A list of any sentences you could not improve and why

## Bundled resources
- `./banned-phrases.md` — full dictionary of phrases to strip, with rationale

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero banned phrases remain (verify against `./banned-phrases.md`)
- Zero em dashes
- Zero emoji in body copy
- Every paragraph has at least one concrete verb or number
- Voice keywords detectable across the copy
